summary_one_line: A valid sfnt header (00 01 00 00) with a GPOS/GSUB table tag at offset 0x0c so layout parsing reaches get_feature_variations()

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Input must be a well-formed OpenType/sfnt font: 4-byte sfnt version magic 00 01 00 00 at offset 0x00, followed by a table-directory entry whose 4-byte tag at offset 0x0c is the ASCII FOURCC 'GPOS' or 'GSUB'. Only a structurally valid GSUB/GPOS table lets shaping descend through find_variations_index -> get_feature_variations and execute the default arm at line 4283.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed begins with the exact sfnt magic 00 01 00 00 (offsets 0x00-0x03 hold 00 01 00 00 in all 20 W samples) and carries an ASCII table tag GPOS or GSUB at offset 0x0c (W concentrates 47 'G' x20, 50 'P'/53 'S', 4f 'O', 53 'S'/42 'B'). These magic/tag bytes are CMP-gated equality comparisons against the literal table tags, satisfied by I2S substitution.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are short and structurally invalid: offset 0x0c carries scattered bytes (00/01/20/2c, +11 unique) with no GPOS/GSUB FOURCC, and the sfnt magic offsets 0x00-0x03 hold heterogeneous bytes (e0/05/87/0e...). Without the magic+tag the parser never reaches the version switch, so the default branch stays unhit and L blocks 10/10.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog/vpc I2SRandReplace reads the CmpLogObserver operands from the sfnt-magic and FOURCC tag-table equality comparisons and substitutes the exact required literals (00 01 00 00, 'GPOS'/'GSUB') at the matching offsets in one mutation, jumping a multi-byte equality wall that naive/value_profile havoc cannot guess; the dash-row I2S floor in the winning cmplog seed's lineage confirms I2S participation.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the default arm of a switch on the table version major, reached only via the feature-variations lookup call chain.
Reaching the blocker requires descending from find_variations_index into get_feature_variations.
Winner seeds begin with the sfnt version magic 00 01 00 00.
Winner seeds carry an ASCII GPOS table tag in the header region.
A winner seed carries an ASCII GSUB table tag instead, both tags satisfy the same gate.
At offset 0x0c the winner bytes are concentrated on G/P/S (table-tag FOURCC) while loser bytes are scattered with no FOURCC.
Loser-blocking seeds are short and lack the sfnt magic and table tags.
cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a matched CMP operand at the offsets where the other operand appears.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so the vpc>vp pair isolates the same I2S substitution.
Both decisive pairs differ by the I2S technique.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 5555 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 002c9ad92acce91b` and observed 2 I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depth 29 and 30 of the 31-level ancestor chain, lower-bound evidence of I2SRandReplace contribution under the current build

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating the default arm behind the sfnt magic + GPOS/GSUB FOURCC equality where naive/value_profile resolve it at the same rate as cmplog/vpc, showing the multi-byte tag wall is not what separates I2S from non-I2S.

weakest_evidence_point: Whether the FOURCC tag at offset 0x0c is itself the CMP-gated wall versus merely a downstream correlate of a deeper table-directory length/offset check that I2S actually solves.
